claims.statement: AirDNA reports 541 active listings, $24.1K average trailing revenue, 43% available-night occupancy, $243 achieved ADR, and $100 RevPAR through June 2026; supply rose while each performance measure fell year over year.

Each nightly-rental property requires a city endorsement matching its physical location and a building/safety inspection before operation; zoning, occupancy/parking, current endorsement count, and property eligibility remain unknown.
Redfin reports a $370,178 all-home median over the three months ending May 2026, down 11.9% year over year; this is not a clean nightly-rental purchase sample.
Grays Harbor is one of five counties in the Peninsulas research region, but the linked report values and local getting-here page were unavailable; no feeder volume or drive-time value is published.

Freddie Mac's U.S. 30-year average was 6.55% on July 16, 2026, while Washington's 2025 overnight, international, and hotel-demand context softened; neither source is an Ocean Shores property quote or forecast.

The city reports 23 hotel properties and a 29,900-square-foot convention center with 16,900 square feet of flexible space and group capacity from 30 to 1,000; no room count or hotel performance was obtained.
The July 2026 city calendar lists convention and public events with organizer attendance estimates, but no event is assigned a causal lodging effect and calendar gaps do not mean zero demand.

The city charges 0.2% B&O on gross receipts and applies water, sewer-volume, and storm billing rules, but no property bill, insurance quote, association status, septic status, reserve, or fixed annual amount was obtained.

Washington coast tsunami planning, FEMA address-level flood maps, separate flood insurance, localized access closures, wind/storm, salt/moisture, association, utility, and septic diligence are material, but no citywide property value, premium, probability, or downtime assumption is supportable.
Agent-authored remaining-2026 base assumes demand down 4%, supply up 3%, ADR down 1%, 167 calendar nights, and no invented downtime; coastal days and costs require user inputs.
Simultaneous broad performance declines and expanding active supply make continued near-term softness plausible, subject to seasonal and access uncertainty.
Falsify the remaining-2026 base if two consecutive aligned monthly updates show occupancy and RevPAR above prior-year levels while active supply growth slows below demand growth.
Agent-authored 2027 downside assumes demand down 10%, supply up 5%, ADR down 5%, 365 calendar nights, and no default coastal cost or downtime.
The downside remains plausible if supply keeps expanding, Washington overnight travel remains weak, and local coastal burdens constrain availability or costs.
Falsify the downside if source-aligned 2027 occupancy and RevPAR recover above June 2026 levels for two consecutive quarters while active supply growth moderates.
Agent-authored 2027 base assumes demand up 1%, supply up 2%, ADR up 1%, 365 calendar nights, and user-entered coastal downtime and costs.
A weak stabilization is plausible only if the national 2027 occupancy recovery reaches this market and supply growth no longer outruns demand.
Falsify the base if two consecutive quarters show RevPAR still falling more than 10% year over year or supply growth above demand growth.
Agent-authored 2027 upside assumes demand up 6%, flat supply, ADR up 4%, 365 calendar nights, and explicit user coastal inputs.
An upside requires aligned broad recovery and supply discipline; event presence alone is insufficient evidence.
Falsify the upside if 2027 Q1 and Q2 aligned occupancy or RevPAR fail to exceed their 2026 counterparts or supply growth remains positive without stronger demand.

claims.statement: No public source fetched in this invocation supplies a dated, auditable Ocean Shores 5+ entire-home luxury occupancy, ADR, RevPAR, or revenue series with qualifying sample n; all observed segment fields remain unknown.

AirDNA reports 1.3% 5+ bedrooms and 86.5% entire homes as separate broad marginals, while the city requires one endorsement and inspection per nightly-rental property; no intersection or legal luxury count is available.

The July 18 Redfin page exposes four active 5+ house asks from $405,900 to $879,000; the sample is sparse, unverified, and not audited for rental status, legal overlap, premium attributes, or achieved performance.
Peninsulas methodology and Grays Harbor membership are known, but no Ocean Shores luxury feeder, distance, or drive-time value was obtained and the local access page returned 404.
The U.S. 6.55% 30-year conventional average is context only; a large coastal house requires property- and borrower-specific financing.
The city reports 23 hotels, but no room inventory or large-group hotel performance is available for comparison with a 5+ house.
City events and tourism descriptions identify group-use, beachfront, canal-front, and neighborhood contexts, but no event or amenity receives an observed luxury rate or occupancy effect.

Property tax, endorsement/inspection, homeowner/STR and flood insurance, wind/storm reserve, salt/moisture maintenance, water, sewer volume, storm drain, association, septic, and maintenance require explicit property inputs; none is zero or observed here.
Coastal access, flood, wind, salt/moisture, insurance, utility, association, and septic effects remain property-specific unknowns; official sources do not provide a citywide default.
The only segment scenario requires user base occupancy, ADR, purchase price, variable cost, fixed-risk cost, and downtime; it publishes no observed segment forecast.

Replace the unknown segment posture only when an accessible auditable 5+ entire-home qualifying sample supplies achieved performance, sample n, legal overlap, property coastal inputs, and matching definitions.

limitations: No observed segment forecast or default economics.
